data.title: HSBC Names New Hong Kong CEO in Leadership Shuffle
data.description:
By Kimberley Kao 




  HSBC Holdings is shuffling its leadership ranks in Hong Kong, naming Maggie Ng as chief executive to succeed Luanne Lim, who will take the top job at Hang Seng Bank. 



  The global lender said Thursday that Ng, who joined in 2020, will retain her role as head of retail banking and wealth for Hong Kong. The changes are effective in October, subject to regulatory approval. 



  Lim, a more than 25-year veteran at the bank, will head its separately listed subsidiary at a time when the Hong Kong lender is facing declining profits and higher credit losses amid continuing economic uncertainties. 



  As part of the shuffle, Hang Seng Bank CEO Diana Cesar will move to a new position as vice chairman of HSBC Hong Kong, where she will advise the co-chiefs of Asia and the Middle East. 



  The leadership changes in Hong Kong are the latest under HSBC CEO Georges Elhedery, who has shaken up the London-based lender's bureaucratic structure and axed layers of management to simplify the bank's structure since taking over in 2024. 



  Over the past year, HSBC has merged its commercial- and investment-banking operations, cut the size of its executive committee by a third, and announced plans to stop advising companies on doing deals and going public in the U.S. and Europe. Elhedery has also made operations in HSBC's biggest markets, the U.K. and Hong Kong, as standalone businesses. 



  HSBC currently holds a nearly 64% stake in Hang Seng Bank. 



  Shares of HSBC in Hong Kong were 0.8% higher at midday Friday, while Hang Seng Bank's stock was up 0.4%. 





  Write to Kimberley Kao at kimberley.kao@wsj.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  September 05, 2025 00:50 ET (04:50 GMT)


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: EMEA Morning Briefing: Investors Await U.S. Jobs Report
data.description:
MARKET WRAPS

Watch For:

EU GDP; U.K. retail sales; Germany manufacturing orders; no major trading updates expected

Opening Call:

European stock futures were higher as were Asia stocks. The dollar weakened, and U.S. Treasurys were flat. Gold rose and oil fell.

Equities:

European stock futures were higher. The big event today is the U.S. August nonfarm payrolls report.

"In the short term, markets may embrace that data because it should increase the odds of Fed rate cuts," said Chris Larkin, managing director, trading and investing at E*TRADE from Morgan Stanley.

"But if the numbers deteriorate too much, it could raise concerns about the health of the economy."

Forex:

The dollar remains modestly overvalued even after easing significantly this year but it could become undervalued next year, Bank of America analysts said.

The dollar has converged rapidly closer towards fair value this year and for less conventional reasons such as German fiscal stimulus, the trade war and the risks to U.S. institutional integrity such as the threat to the Federal Reserve's independence.

Continued erosion of U.S. institutions could drive the dollar past fair value and towards historical undervaluation, they said.

Bonds:

Fresh data backed up a decline in U.S. job creation, driving investors to the safety of Treasurys and pushing yields lower.

An interest rate cut by the Fed in a couple of weeks is priced as nearly certain, after ADP reported lower-than-expected new jobs in August and weekly jobless claims climbed more than forecast.

August payrolls are likely to confirm the trend and show unemployment inching up, according to economists surveyed by WSJ.

Energy:

Oil fell with sentiment weighed by increasing concerns of oversupply, Westpac analysts said.

The market will remain on edge ahead of this weekend's meeting of OPEC producers about possible output increases, the ANZ Research team said.

Metals:

Gold edged higher as traders await the U.S. nonfarm payrolls data due later today.

The data should help shape the Fed's rate path trajectory. The market has also locked in profits, as gold's rally was fueled by strong haven demand and Fed rate cuts expectations, ANZ Research analysts said.

A Fed rate cut would further boost noninterest-bearing bullion's appeal.

-

Base metals were mixed, trading in a tight range. Prices are likely capped by a strong U.S. dollar ahead of Friday's U.S. payrolls data, the Saxo APAC Research team said.

For copper, investors are likely weighing concerns of weaker demand in China.

EV maker BYD cut its sales targets for the year over intense competition and supply-side issues after mining company Codelco said its Chile copper production could stagnate over operational challenges, said the ANZ Research team.

-

Iron ore futures were mixed as steel production resumed in northern China after Beijing's military parade.

Despite the temporary production halt, Chinese steel mills' margins have been lower in recent weeks, which suggests deteriorating steel demand, said CBA's Vivek Dhar.

"We anticipate that China's policymakers will announce additional stimulus measures in late 3Q or early 4Q that help China's commodity--intensive economy," he said.

TODAY'S TOP HEADLINES

Trump aide chosen for Fed board clears first hurdle as questions swirl about central bank's independence

President Donald Trump's effort to remake the Federal Reserve and push the central bank to cut interest rates gained momentum after a top White House adviser nominated to fill an open seat on the Fed board appeared to win the endorsement of Republican senators.

Stephen Miran, one of the president's key economic advisers, promised senators he would be an independent voice on the Fed and not be beholden to Trump. His answers left Democrats very skeptical.

Trump Prepares to Start North American Trade Deal Renegotiation

The U.S. is preparing to start renegotiating its largest free trade deal-the U.S.-Mexico-Canada Agreement.

Within the next month, the Office of the U.S. Trade Representative will begin public consultations on renegotiating the deal, which it must do by Oct. 4 under the 2020 law that implemented the pact. A request for comment from companies and unions could be issued as soon as this week, say people familiar with the administration's thinking, though President Trump's team has previously indicated to stakeholders that a request was close, before pushing back the release date.

What's Next for Giorgio Armani's Fashion Empire?

When Giorgio Armani died at 91, the fashion world lost not only a designer but one of the last great owner-operators in the luxury industry.

Unlike rivals who ceded control to conglomerates such as LVMH or Kering, Armani kept his company private, answering only to himself and running it with the precision of a businessman as much as the eye of a couturier. Fiercely protective of that independence, he remained effectively the sole shareholder of a group that generated about $2.7 billion in sales last year, and built safeguards to ensure the house would resist an outside takeover.

Israeli Security Officials Raise Concerns About Gaza City Offensive

Top Israeli security officials and ministers are privately urging Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu to do a temporary cease-fire deal with Hamas rather than expand a ground offensive against the militant group in Gaza City, according to Israeli officials.

In a fiery six-hour security cabinet meeting Sunday night, Israeli military chief Eyal Zamir warned Netanyahu and other senior ministers present that the operation could lead to Israeli military rule over Gaza, some of the officials said.

Broadcom Swings to Profit, Beats on Sales Outlook

Broadcom swung to a profit and surpassed expectations for fiscal third-quarter sales and its outlook for the current quarter.

The semiconductors and software maker on Thursday posted a profit of $4.14 billion, or 85 cents a share, in the quarter ended in early August, compared with a loss of $1.88 billion, or 40 cents a share, a year earlier.

Record EV Sales Drive Auto Market Ahead of Credit End

Electric vehicles are propelling auto sales in the U.S.-but not for long.

New-vehicle sales rose 3.7% in August from a year earlier, helped by consumers' rush to lock in expiring EV tax credits and steady prices in the face of tariffs.

Write to singaporeeditors@dowjones.com

06:00/UK: Jul UK monthly retail sales figures

data.title: Jalil Lopez Announces "Metamorfosis," a Bold and Soulful New Chapter Arriving September 2025
data.description:
Jalil Lopez Announces "Metamorfosis," a Bold and Soulful New Chapter Arriving September 2025

Jalil Lopez Announces "Metamorfosis," a Bold and Soulful New Chapter Arriving September 2025

PR Newswire

MIAMI, Sept. 5, 2025

MIAMI, Sept. 5, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- With a voice shaped by loss and lifted by faith, Jalil Lopez today announces the release of Metamorfosis, an intimate and genre-blending seven-track EP, plus a bonus track, that traces his path from heartbreak to healing. Slated for release on Friday, September 5th via RMG, Metamorfosis marks a new creative high for the Newark-born artist whose music bridges generations and genres, from classic Bachata to contemporary urban soul and hip hop.

 "This project is a reflection of everything I've lived through..."

"This project is a reflection of everything I've lived through, what nearly broke me, what and who brought me back, and how I've grown through it all," says Lopez. "It's my truth, and I hope it allows me to connect with my fans all around the world in unique ways."

Metamorfosis is a transformative seven track EP (with a floor-shaking bonus track) featuring work from the likes of three-time Latin Grammy Nominated and 2024 Latin Grammy Winner, for Song of the Year, Andres Rios, Latin Grammy-nominated, Jorge Milano and Lil' Genuiz, and four-time Latin Grammy Winner, Alejandro "Mosty" Patiño.

The EP blends the rich emotion of traditional Bachata with the grit and honesty of Lopez's urban upbringing. His lyrics dig deep, exploring themes of grief, redemption, self-discovery, heartache, and joy, always underscored by a vulnerability that resonates with listeners the world over.

Building on the viral global success of his recent singles "No Vas a Encontrar" featuring RKM, "La Culpable," and "Borracho y Loco," Lopez is entering into a bold new chapter. Known for early hits like "Princesa Mía" and "Tu Primer Amor," as well as genre-defying collaborations with artists like Rick Ross and DJ Khaled, he has always embraced evolution. But Metamorfosis marks a turning point. More than just a project, it represents an inflection point and transformation into the man he's become.

Metamorfosis mirrors Lopez's profound journey of transformation, one marked by resilience and renewed purpose. The EP captures the artist's growth through both struggle and triumph, with every lyric and melody revealing a deeper layer of his story. Rooted in honesty and driven by passion, Lopez emerges stronger and more authentic than ever, delivering a bold new voice to the ever-evolving sound of Latin music.

Metamorfosis will be available on all major streaming platforms on Friday, September 5, 2025.

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/jalil-lopez-announces-metamorfosis-a-bold-and-soulful-new-chapter-arriving-september-2025-302547040.html

SOURCE Redline Media Group

data.title: BitMine Immersion (BMNR) Statement on NYSE American Listing and Access to Capital Markets
data.description:
BitMine Immersion (BMNR) Statement on NYSE American Listing and Access to Capital Markets

BitMine Immersion (BMNR) Statement on NYSE American Listing and Access to Capital Markets

PR Newswire

LAS VEGAS, Sept. 5, 2025

BitMine, the leading Ethereum treasury, listed on July 8, 2025, fully compliant with NYSE standards and guidelines

LAS VEGAS, Sept. 5, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- (NYSE AMERICAN: BMNR) BitMine Immersion Technologies ("BitMine" or the "Company") today released this statement regarding several media reports that NASDAQ is stepping up scrutinization of crypto treasury companies.

BitMine is listed on the NYSE American.

BitMine is able to issue shares via its existing shelf registration without shareholder approval.

The existing ATM program remains a registered bona fide public deal and does not require shareholder approval to continue.

Media reports claimed that NASDAQ is requiring some companies to get shareholder approval before issuing new shares to buy crypto. This has been the existing guidance by NASDAQ and relates to companies establishing crypto treasuries and accepting crypto in exchange for new shares (so-called "in-kind contribution"). Those exceeding 20% in an offering would require shareholder approval. 

This does not apply to BitMine, which has already secured NYSE approval, with its PIPE (private investment in public equity) transaction closing on July 8, 2025.

The company recently released a corporate presentation, which can be found here: https://bitminetech.io/investor-relations/

To stay informed, please sign up at: https://bitminetech.io/contact-us/

About BitMine
BitMine is a Bitcoin and Ethereum Network Company with a focus on the accumulation of Crypto for long term investment, whether acquired by our Bitcoin mining operations or from the proceeds of capital raising transactions. Company business lines include Bitcoin Mining, synthetic Bitcoin mining through involvement in Bitcoin mining, hashrate as a financial product, offering advisory and mining services to companies interested in earning Bitcoin denominated revenues, and general Bitcoin advisory to public companies. BitMine's operations are located in low-cost energy regions in Trinidad; Pecos, Texas; and Silverton, Texas.

For additional details, follow on X:
https://x.com/bitmnr
https://x.com/fundstrat
https://x.com/bmnrintern

Forward Looking Statements
This press release contains statements that constitute "forward-looking statements." The statements in this press release that are not purely historical are forward-looking statements which involve risks and uncertainties. This document specifically contains forward-looking statements regarding progress and achievement of the Company's goals regarding ETH acquisition and staking, the long-term value of Ethereum, continued growth and advancement of the Company's Ethereum treasury strategy and the applicable benefits to the Company. In evaluating these forward-looking statements, you should consider various factors, including BitMine's ability to keep pace with new technology and changing market needs; BitMine's ability to finance its current business, Ethereum treasury operations and proposed future business; the competitive environment of BitMine's business; and the future value of Bitcoin and Ethereum. Actual future performance outcomes and results may differ materially from those expressed in forward-looking statements. Forward-looking statements are subject to numerous conditions, many of which are beyond BitMine's control, including those set forth in the Risk Factors section of BitMine's Form 10-K filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission (the "SEC") on April 3, 2025, as well as all other SEC filings, as amended or updated from time to time. Copies of BitMine's filings with the SEC are available on the SEC's website at www.sec.gov. BitMine undertakes no obligation to update these statements for revisions or changes after the date of this release, except as required by law.

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/bitmine-immersion-bmnr-statement-on-nyse-american-listing-and-access-to-capital-markets-302547414.html

SOURCE BitMine Immersion Technologies, Inc.

data.title: Notice of Data Incident
data.description:
Notice of Data Incident

Notice of Data Incident

PR Newswire

September 4

Nashville, TN – BFC Solutions experienced a network disruption that affected its ability to access certain systems. In response, BFC Solutions promptly initiated an investigation, engaging third-party specialists to assist with understanding the nature and scope of the disruption. As part of the investigation, it was discovered that certain information within BFC Solutions systems was subject to unauthorized access or acquisition between June 18, 2024 and June 20, 2024. Upon discovery, BFC Solutions worked to identify and collect the data at risk to perform a thorough review to determine the types of information that may have been impacted and the individuals to whom it relates. On June 11, 2025, this process was completed, and BFC Solutions worked to confirm up-to-date contact information to provide individuals with notification as soon as possible. The review determined that the types of information potentially impacted, which varies by individual, may include one or more of the following: name, date of birth, driver's license/state issued identification number, passport number, financial account information, payment card information, medical information, health insurance information, and/or Social Security number.

In response to this incident, BFC Solutions engaged outside cybersecurity specialists to conduct an investigation. BFC Solutions also continues to review its policies and procedures related to data protection. BFC Solutions has no reason to believe any information has been or will be misused because of this incident. In an abundance of caution, potentially affected individuals are being provided with credit monitoring services at no cost to them. If you have questions about this incident, please call 1-833-426-3695 between the hours of 8:00 a.m. to 8:00 p.m. Eastern time, Monday through Friday, excluding holidays. BFC Solutions can also be reached in writing at 545 Mainstream Dr, Nashville, TN 37228.

In general, individuals are encouraged to remain vigilant against incidents of identity theft and fraud by reviewing credit reports and account statements for suspicious activity and to detect errors. Under U.S. law, individuals are entitled to one free credit report annually from each of the three major credit reporting bureaus, TransUnion, Experian, and Equifax. To order your free credit report, visit www.annualcreditreport.com or call 1-877-322-8228. Individuals may also place a fraud alert or credit freeze by contacting the credit reporting agencies: TransUnion 1-800-680-7289, P.O. Box 2000 Chester, PA 19016, transunion.com; Experian 1-888-397-3742, P.O. Box 9554 Allen, TX 75013, experian.com; Equifax 1-888-298-0045, P.O. Box 105069 Atlanta, GA 30348, equifax.com. Individuals can further educate themselves regarding identity theft, fraud alerts, credit freezes, and steps to protect their personal information by contacting the credit reporting bureaus, the Federal Trade Commission ("FTC"), or their state Attorney General. The FTC may be reached at 600 Pennsylvania Ave. NW, Washington, D.C. 20580; www.identitytheft.gov; 1-877-ID-THEFT (1-877-438-4338); and TTY: 1-866-653-4261.

/PRNewswire -- Sept. 4, 2025/

SOURCE BFC Solutions
